title: Hosted viewer must hydrate compact memory graph
summary: The hosted GitHub Pages viewer must load ./data/kage/graph.json through loadGraphPath, not fetchJson. The published graph is compact and stores memory entities/edges/episodes in refs; raw fetchJson leaves those refs unre
body:
The hosted GitHub Pages viewer must load ./data/kage/graph.json through loadGraphPath, not fetchJson. The published graph is compact and stores memory entities/edges/episodes in refs; raw fetchJson leaves those refs unresolved and memory nodes disappear even when the split files are deployed.
Evidence: User reported no memory nodes on deployed viewer after entities.json, edges.json, and episodes.json were published. loadHostedDefault used fetchJson for graph.json while URL/local graph loading used loadGraphPath.
Verified by: npm --prefix mcp test
